AC: 32
STR: +5 DEX: +4 WIS: +14 HP: +60
SV FIRE: +1 SV DISEASE: +1 SV COLD: +1 SV MAGIC: +1 SV POISON: +1
WT: 7.0 Size: LARGE
Class: RNG
Race: HUM ELF HEF HFL DRK
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Slot 2, Type 21 (Special Ornamentation)

Expansion: Scars of Velious
Rarity: Rare
Level to Attain: 50
[Comments ]

Expansion List - Premium only.
Quest Name
Ranger Kael Armor

Zone(s) Found In:

hard choice,most rangers will say thurgadin, cuz it offers the most hps. kael legs give most ac ,but not much rangers look for just ac. thurg gives 10 sta 60 hps, though far less ac,while tolans ,mmm, well no hps or saves there, only the dex might favor those (but only slightly.P)
i wonder, why didn't you ask for skyshrine legs as well ?

▲ Page top
Summarry of thread -- and a few notes
QuoteReply
# Nov 06 2002 at 2:45 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1592252462989
View User Forum
Sage
211 posts
Hello all --

In an effort to save time I have consolodated a few notes from the pages of text below.

1. Yes, Halfling rangers can equip these. The screen shot is pre-halfling-ranger era, and Illia will update if one of us send in a photo.

2. Yes, wonderful AC and splendid Wisdom. One of the few armour items that us mid-level warders have that bears such a lovely wisdom score.

3. Yes, there is a soft cap for AC. Most level 60 rangers try to shoot for about 1100/1200 AC and then work either hp, saves, or stats depending on their playstyle and hunting locations. This item is a critical piece for giant faction rangers as the 32 AC can not be overlooked easily.

4. Yes Druids need wisdom. No, these are not druid useable (/bonk bewielders me ...)

5. These are giant faction quested legs. There are 3 major faction groups -- Dorfs, giants and dragons. Giants are further divided into Kromiff, Kromzek, and Tormax faction. In order to do this quest, you will need to get your Kromzek faction to warmly, and it is pretty smart for you to get your Tormax faction to at least Dubious. Kromiff faction is not required ( I was KOS at the time of my turn in), but it makes it a lot easier for you to get around in Kael if you are not concerned about all the Kromiff trash bonking you. (On a side note, I found it a small challenge and a lot of fun attempting to sneek around and camoing trying to get past them all -- the journey is half the fun :-) /giggle)

6. For more details on the quest check out the quest and the required gemmies for each item listed above.

7. For improving your faction there are a series of different quests that you can do to impove your giant factions. It is theorectically possible to become Ally to all 3 Kings (Dorf, CoV, and Tormax) it is very very difficult to maintain these factions if you are in a raiding guild. (To do this Kill giants to improve/max your COV and Dorf fctions and then kill Velks the Sorc about 40 times .. difficult, but not impossible) Most people find that hunting the CoV dragons and drakes while hoping for a set of chain to drop is generally enough. Killing some (100) dorfs, turning in their heads(30 or so) and turning in Kobald paws (eeks I had turned in too many to count) from Velks lab will generally finsh your faction hits off to the point where you can complete the quest.

8. The Kael armour items drop in the Temple of Vesh. in the north and west wings. The west wing is commonly hunted for this purpose. There are a couple points in which to pull to, but I like the "zone out" room if LTK is not up. If she is up then we raid her and then do the armour farming :-)).

9. Generally, a well balanced team of 3 velious equiped groups is the number I try to shot for when we do this. Standard mix of 3 MA type, 2 chanter, 2 shammy, 3 cleric as a baseline, with the critical pieces being a CoV faction mage available to CoTH the raiding groups into whatever location you want to pull to, and some form of FD puller. Round out your 18 people with a smattering of rangers rogues, casters and such, and you should be set for a happy hunt.

10. Watch the gating mobs - mobs gting and getting summoned is never a fun way to have a raid end. (Remember white and blue ... white and blue ...)

Just a few notes and comments. Hope this helps someone :-)



▲ Page top

Tanking
QuoteReply
# May 16 2001 at 4:48 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Getting AC higher is a big concern for EVERY class that might have the slightest chance of taking damage... Same with resists... evrything counts for something... and say going up against Cazic-Thule or some other bad beast I'd want every chance of resisting, taking less damage, and all around surviving. This is an effective and simply beefy piece of armor, not much better out there... Suck it up.

Sadly they resemble Ivy.

From what I've seen, just look at Ry'gorr, tint your monitor to full green, almost to the point where you might look like that dr00d who is in your group. Now place your hands around your character. Focus your eyes, and wala: Forest Stalker armor.

#Anonymous, Posted: Mar 10 2001 at 5:25 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) The saves on these leggins are a joke, please make them + 5 at least or just add to the other stats.
RE: Saves blow
QuoteReply
# Mar 11 2001 at 4:11 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592756600730
103 posts
Why must everyone complain about everything? No one will ever be happy in this game unless every piece of equipment they equip will give them +100 to every attribute in the game. Stop whining! IF all your Kael equipment gives you +1 or +2 to saves them that will equal around +5 to +10 which would be very nice. So stop whining about this particular piece of equipment. Many rangers are happy since this piece of equipment gives a nice boost to ac and wisdon, along with some other little touch ups to the rest of your stats. So be happy with what you get, and stop being unhappy with what you don't get!

RE: Saves blow

# Mar 11 2001 at 9:11 PM Rating: Default

View User Forum
Anonymous
I agree with Nin... Also, if you look at most of the rest of the Velious armor (other classes) you will find the same thing. 1-2 to all saves. These are ment to go in a complete set...and with the complete set you will have saves comparable to Planar armor, and perhaps a little more AC.

Tyna 54 Elementalist

#Anonymous, Posted: Mar 10 2001 at 8:46 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Nooooo!!!! I have too much mana as is, I wish Kael BP was effect clarity 2. ***** druids! :p hehe their kael leggings are nice too, and the 32 ac sure does solve alot of defense issues other rangers whine about excessively /glare. Rangers are a class that take CARE in every piece of equipment, I highly recommend picking another class if you want AC and don't have a good guild/team to help you get the gear you need to be able to take some hits from mobs. No matter how hard you jolt at high levels, you'll sometimes be putting out way too much damage and will need to tank. As for everyone else that calls rangers horrible 'tanks'... heh, I'll see you in the fields :) /rant off ;p
